programme.title: Live: Unsportsmanlike with Evan, Canty and Michelle
programme.desc: Start the day off right with Chris Canty, Evan Cohen and Michelle Smallmon providing an energizing and informative way to take on the day. Sports, life and laughter are what it's all about.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The No. 18 Hurricanes seek a bounce-back effort when they host the Orange. Miami's College Football Playoff hopes have taken a hit after losing two of the past three games. Syracuse disrupted Miami's 2024 CFP run with a 42-38 upset Nov. 30, 2024.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The Wolfpack set out to play spoiler once more by visiting the No. 15 Hurricanes. NC State upset then-No. 8 Georgia Tech 48-36 prior to its bye Nov. 1. Freshman Jayden Scott (NCSU) scored and set a new career high with 196 rushing yards in the win.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The No. 13 Hurricanes (8-2, 4-2 ACC) seek their fifth straight victory over the Hokies (3-7, 2-4). Miami has won three of its last four games following a 41-7 win over NC State. Virginia Tech quarterback Kyron Drones has accounted for 25 touchdowns.

programme.title: College Football
programme.desc: The No. 22 Panthers (8-3, 6-1) host the No. 12 Hurricanes (9-2, 5-2) in a clash of ACC title game contenders. Pitt needs a victory, and a loss by SMU or Virginia to play for the championship. Miami must win, and have Virginia Tech win or Duke lose.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: Filmmaker Billy Corben documents the rise to prominence of the college football program at the University of Miami.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The University of Miami's football program tries to recover from the devastation left by NCAA sanctions and scandals.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The fascinating backstory behind the famous/infamous 1988 "Catholics vs. Convicts" college football game between Notre Dame and the University of Miami. The slogan summarized the subtext of cultural and racial tensions brewing between the schools.

programme.title: 30 for 30
programme.desc: The polarizing beliefs and on-field successes of former Colorado football coach Bill McCartney.

programme.desc: At the 1983 College World Series, the championship culminated in a matchup between Roger Clemens' Texas Longhorns and the upstart Alabama Crimson Tide; a team of overachievers that included the best hitter in college baseball, Dave Magadan.

programme.desc: In search of Old Florida, we walk the ice boxes at Kyle's Seafood to survey the catch and eat smoked mullet on the bed of a pickup; drinking beer and talking about home at a beach bar with local author C.H. Hooks.

programme.title: Live: Get Up
programme.desc: ESPN's morning show features host Mike Greenberg with a rotating and diverse group of analysts opining about and analyzing the latest news from the world of sports. The two-hour, personality-driven show originates from Hudson Square in Lower Manhattan, a modern media hub for ESPN and The Walt Disney Company's New York-based teams. Greenberg, aka "Greeny," is formerly one-half of ESPN Radio's longt

programme.title: Live: First Take
programme.desc: Stephen A. Smith hosts this weekday morning sports debate show. Smith provides his expert takes on the day's trending sports topics and under-the-radar news. They're regularly joined by veteran analysts and the top guests in sports and entertainment.

programme.title: Live: The Pat McAfee Show
programme.desc: Former NFL punter Pat McAfee brings his humor and candor to the microphone to highlight the day's top stories.

programme.title: Live: SportsCenter
programme.desc: The latest news, highlights and inside information from every corner of the sports world is presented each day on "SportsCenter," ESPN's first-ever program when the network debuted in September of 1979. programme.title: NFL Live

programme.title: Live: Pardon the Interruption
programme.desc: The debate is the story in this fast-paced, half-hour series hosted by former Washington Post sportswriters Tony Kornheiser and Michael Wilbon. The pair engage in high-energy discussions, conduct interviews and often argue over topics not even related to sports. Other sports reporters appear as guests and offer opinions on the top stories of the day.
